articles.title: How to Watch Google I/O 2026
articles.why: Google I/O 2026 is one of tech's most watched annual events — with Android XR smart glasses and Gemini updates on the agenda, this is the practical guide to a keynote that will generate significant news today.
articles.hook: Google I/O is today. / Smart glasses, Gemini, Android overhaul. / Here's what's actually coming.

articles.title: Australian Aboriginals cared for a dingo's grave for decades
articles.why: A thousand-year-old dingo burial maintained by Indigenous Australians for decades is a rare archaeology story that reframes how we understand human-animal bonds — and the long continuity of Indigenous care for country. Quietly resonant.
articles.hook: They buried a dingo 1,000 years ago. / And kept tending the grave for decades.

articles.title: Did Artemis II break through? Registrations at Space Camp double afterward.
articles.why: Space Camp registrations doubling after Artemis II is a quiet but meaningful data point — it suggests the lunar program may be generating genuine public inspiration rather than just bureaucratic momentum. Cultural signal for anyone tracking the long arc of space enthusiasm.
articles.hook: Artemis II flew. / Space Camp registrations doubled. / Something is working.

articles.title: PSA: The first F1 race on Netflix is this weekend
articles.why: The first F1 race on Netflix this weekend is a genuine media landmark — Formula 1 completing its journey from niche sport to Netflix-era cultural juggernaut, now broadcasting live on the platform that helped create its new audience.
articles.hook: F1's first Netflix race is this weekend. / Drive to Survive built the audience. / Now they get to watch.

articles.title: How to watch the Google I/O 2026 keynote
articles.why: Google I/O 2026 is tomorrow and it's arriving at the most competitive moment in AI's history — with OpenAI, Apple, and Anthropic all making moves this month. What Google announces will define whether it's leading or chasing.
articles.hook: Google I/O is tomorrow. / Every AI company is watching.
